reviews.comment:
The online course was very comprehensive and informative. There were a couple of glitches, however. The first time through the course took about 4.5 hours and after answering the last question, it took me back to the beginning and said I had completed 0%. Since I had no proof I had finished, I had to retake it the next day. Then I noticed that when I had to step away to take care of something, it booted me back several pages which I had to repeat.
The in-person training with Brian Ennis was excellent. He is very knowledgeable and put me completely at ease.

reviewReply.comment:
Thank you so much for taking the time to share your feedback with us. We genuinely appreciate your thoughtful review and the Stars you've awarded us.

We're truly sorry to hear about the frustrations you encountered during your online course experience. It's disheartening to learn about the glitches you faced, especially after investing your valuable time and effort. While such hiccups are uncommon, we understand that they can be incredibly frustrating, and we recognize that reassurances don't quite make up for the time lost.

Regrettably, while AHA strives for seamless online experiences, local limitations sometimes restrict our control over such issues. However, to ensure smoother learning experiences for our participants, attending classes in-person assure our control of the delivery.

Looking ahead, we're excited about the prospect of working with you again in the future. To provide you with a more reliable and hassle-free experience, we'd like to extend an invitation to join us for future classes in-person. This way, we can ensure that you receive the highest quality training without any eLearning challenges.

For your convenience, below are links to our upcoming in-person BLS and CPR/AED training sessions.  These dates will update as you near your renewal dates.

   BLS in-person: https://coastalcprtraining.enrollware.com/schedule#ct359474

   CPR/AED In-person: https://coastalcprtraining.enrollware.com/schedule#ct209809

Once again, we appreciate your feedback, and we're committed to making your future experiences with us exceptional. If you have any questions or concerns, please don't hesitate to reach out.

Thank you, and we hope you have a fantastic weekend!

reviews.comment:
Amy had to improvise because the television she wanted to use to play the video had some technical issues.  She did the teaching section without the video.  Both I and the other student passed our written & the practical despite the technical issue.
I did suggest training in administering narcan because there are civilians who may read it in the manual, but should be trained through the class and practicals.

reviewReply.comment:
Thank you so much for taking the time to share your experience and for your kind understanding regarding the technical issues we encountered during the class. We're thrilled to hear that both you and the other student successfully passed your written and practical exams despite the unforeseen challenges.

We're especially grateful for your suggestion regarding training in administering Narcan. It's an important topic, and we're glad that Amy was able to cover it during the class. While the AHA doesn't require Narcan skills review for course completion, we understand the importance of familiarization with Naloxone Nasal Spray Training Device. We strive to provide sufficient hands-on experience to ensure our students feel confident in emergency situations.

Once again, we truly appreciate your support and feedback. Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any further questions or suggestions.
